news.title: Aadhar Housing Finance's net profit rises by 20.18% to ₹193.40 crore in FY26
news.para: Aadhar Housing Finance achieved a significant 20.18 percent growth in its net consolidated profit for the financial year 2025-26. The company's profit after tax reached ₹1,095.88 crore. Total income also saw a healthy increase of 18.59 percent. Aadhar Housing Finance crossed the ₹30,000 crore AUM milestone.

news.title: Phase II of Ayodhya master plan notified, 766 sq km of three districts covered
news.para: Ayodhya's development authority has unveiled a vast master plan, spanning 766.39 sq km across Ayodhya, Basti, and Gonda districts. This ambitious project incorporates residential, commercial, and entertainment sectors, notably preserving 1,200 natural ponds.  It also plans highway facility zones to foster economic growth and integrate neighboring villages for future expansion.

news.title: Lucknow development body revises Atal Nagar housing scheme payment terms
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) has revised payment terms for its Atal Nagar housing scheme, extending the instalment plan to 10 years with a reduced interest rate of 7%.  Registration for 630 unsold flats, including 1 BHK and 2 BHK units, is open from April 18 to May 17, 2026.

news.title: WeWork India adds 1.1 lakh sq ft workspace at Aerocity in Delhi
news.para: WeWork India has expanded its presence in Delhi-NCR with the launch of a 1.1 lakh sq ft flexible workspace at Aerocity, taking its overall portfolio beyond 1.21 lakh desks across eight cities, amid continued growth in demand for managed office spaces.

news.title: Flexible workspace stock likely to grow 16-18% to 140-145 million sq ft in FY27-FY28: Crisil
news.para: The flexible workspace market in India is gearing up for a robust expansion, with an expected capacity surge of 16-18% by FY28, aiming for 145 million sq ft. Strong demand from various sectors is driving this growth, as operators eye new spaces, particularly in tier II cities. Occupancy rates remain consistent, and profitability forecasts seem favorable.

news.title: MPCB issues closure notices to 84 RMC units in Pune and Pimpri Chinchwad
news.para: Maharashtra Pollution Control Board has taken strong action against ready mix concrete units in Pune and Pimpri Chinchwad. Many units faced closure notices and directions to fix pollution issues. Stricter norms were introduced, leading to temporary scaling back of inspections. Municipal bodies also intensified their enforcement. The RMC Association protested the actions, seeking better coordination and a single-window clearance system.

news.title: India’s real estate equity inflows rise 88% to record $30.7 billion in Q1 2026: Report
news.para: India's real estate sector is booming, attracting $30.7 billion in equity inflows from 2024 to Q1 2026, an 88% surge. Land and office assets led investments, with institutional investors doubling their capital. REITs also saw significant growth, while debt financing exceeded $146 billion, highlighting a robust market expansion.

news.title: Telangana government to take over ₹5,000 crore debt of GHMC
news.para: The Telangana government will assume Rs 5,000 crore in liabilities from the Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC), significantly easing the debt burden on the civic body. This move aims to strengthen GHMC financially, with the state expected to clear these debts by year-end. The liabilities were primarily incurred for infrastructure projects like flyovers and stormwater drains.

news.title: Hyderabad leads South India’s luxury housing market with ₹8,562 crore sales in FY26: Report
news.para: Hyderabad accounted for the highest value of ultra-luxury housing sales in South India during FY26, while Bengaluru recorded the sharpest growth in demand. Chennai remained a smaller, niche market.

news.title: Delhi civic body probes if Vivek Vihar building flouted norms
news.para: Municipal Corporation of Delhi is investigating building bylaw violations following a fatal fire in Vivek Vihar. The fire claimed nine lives. Officials are examining the building plan approval process. Grills on the rear side of the building reportedly hampered rescue efforts. The investigation will also check for excess dwelling units and terrace access. An advisory may be issued to residents.

news.title: VFS Global‑led consortium to launch & maintain 60 model sub‑registrar offices across Maharashtra
news.para: VFS Global and WE Excel Software will establish 60 model sub-registrar offices across Maharashtra over five years to digitally transform property registration. This initiative aims to streamline services and reduce congestion, with offices remaining under government control and staffed by departmental officials. Citizens can opt for these new facilities or continue using standard SRO services.

news.title: Bagmane Prime Office REIT garners ₹1,150 crore from anchor investors
news.para: Bagmane Prime Office REIT has successfully raised approximately Rs 1,150 crore from anchor investors as its Rs 3,405 crore Initial Public Offering (IPO) opens for subscription. The REIT plans to utilize the proceeds to acquire significant office assets in Bengaluru, aiming to expand its portfolio of Grade A+ properties.

news.title: Chandigarh administration rules out CLU, industrial plot conversion
news.para: Chandigarh administration has excluded long-pending demands for converting industrial plots to retail and permitting change of land use (CLU) in villages from its reform agenda. Officials cited a lack of contiguous land, high infrastructure costs, and master plan restrictions as reasons for these decisions, prioritizing industrial development instead.

news.title: About 2,000 notices issued over stilt area coverage violations in Gurugram, HC informed
news.para: Gurugram authorities have issued nearly 2,000 notices to property owners for illegally covering stilt areas. Around 500 restoration orders have also been passed. This information was shared with the Punjab and Haryana high court. The court is hearing a case regarding the 'stilt plus four floor policy' in Gurugram. The policy's implementation remains subject to the ongoing public interest litigation.

news.title: Bombay HC orders Maharashtra government to refund ₹3 lakh stamp duty to homebuyer
news.para: The Bombay High Court has ordered the state government to refund Rs 3 lakh in stamp duty to a homebuyer who mistakenly paid under the wrong head. Despite a slight delay in his refund application, the court ruled that the inadvertent error and unused stamps meant the government couldn't unjustly enrich itself.

news.title: NCLT approves ₹730 crore revival proposal for Rajesh Business & Leisure Hotels
news.para: The National Company Law Tribunal has approved a revival plan for Rajesh Business & Leisure Hotels. A consortium of Rare Asset Reconstruction Company and Check-Inn Hotels will acquire the company. The plan involves a payment of ₹730 crore. This follows the company's admission into insolvency proceedings in April 2022. Secured creditors will receive a full settlement.

news.title: NCLAT ends insolvency proceedings against Embassy Development
news.para: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal has overturned a lower court's decision to start insolvency proceedings against Embassy Development. The tribunal ruled that the case was barred by Section 10A of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code. This section prevents insolvency proceedings for defaults occurring after March 25, 2020.

news.title: Sobha posts net profit of ₹193.40 crore in FY26
news.para: Sobha has announced 104.27 percent surge in its net consolidated profit for the financial year 2025-26. The company's profit after tax reached ₹193.40 crore. Total income also saw an increase of 29.33 percent. The fourth quarter of FY26 demonstrated robust growth with profit after tax rising by 124.80 percent.
